title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Review - Jack OConnell and Ralph Fiennes Fuel Blistering Horror
text: Director Nia DaCosta's film is described as bold, bloody and blisteringly brutal by reviewer Nikki Baughan, praising it as an exhilarating follow-up to last year's 28 Years Later. The narrative picks up immediately after the previous installment, following young Spike as he navigates the infected mainland after leaving his island sanctuary. The success of The Bone Temple lies almost entirely with O'Connell and co-star Ralph Fiennes. Jack O'Connell delivers an unrestrained performance as the psychopathic Sir Jimmy Crystal, while Fiennes returns as Dr. Kelson, described as striking in both appearance and demeanor. The film earns an 18 certificate in the UK, a step up from the previous 15 rating, indicating increased gore and intensity. Supporting cast member Erin Kellyman adds dimension as tough character Jimmy Ink. The cinematography and sound design transform the English landscape into a vicious hellscape. Given the franchise's strong commercial performance, the review anticipates solid returns despite the more mature content potentially limiting younger audience reach.
